summary: reply-router is a runx graph skill for inbound reply handling. It validates sealed original send receipts, grounds unsubscribe suppression in reply text and policy, emits a CAS append_event packet for recipient suppression, routes ordinary replies to a separate governed send-as lane, and blocks unsealed or ambiguous cases for human review without sending.
artifact_summary: reply-router is a runx graph skill that validates a sealed original_send_receipt, classifies inbound reply intent, appends a recipient-keyed suppression event for clear unsubscribe replies, emits a governed routing decision for normal replies, and blocks ambiguous or unsealed cases to a human lane without sending.

items.summary: SKILL.md, X.yaml, classify.mjs, append_event.mjs, emit_route.mjs, dogfood.mjs, and two JSON fixtures are included under skills/reply-router.
classify.mjs returns route=suppress, classification.type=unsubscribe, confidence=0.99, and matched unsubscribe/stop-sending evidence for the sealed fixture.

append_event.mjs emits registry:runx/data-store@0.1.2 append_event with store_id runx.reply-router.suppression.v1, aggregate_id recipient:ana@example.test, expected_version 7, before_version 7, after_version 8, and a deterministic idempotency key.
classify.mjs returns route=needs_agent and no suppression/routing output when original_send_receipt is unsealed.

runx 0.6.14 on this Windows host fails signed receipt-store writes with os error 87 for both this package and the official examples/hello-world harness. This is a local host limitation; the graph profile is kept for hosted/Linux harness replay.

observations.summary: X.yaml declares reply-router version 0.1.0 with default graph runner route and required harness cases.
The sealed unsubscribe fixture classifies as unsubscribe with route=suppress and matched text evidence.
The suppression branch emits registry:runx/data-store@0.1.2 append_event with expected_version and deterministic idempotency key.
The unsealed fixture classifies as needs_agent and does not emit suppression or routing output.
No runner sends a message; route output names a later governed send-as lane only.
Signed receipt writing on this Windows host fails with os error 87 for this package and the official hello-world harness.

dogfood.purpose: Auxiliary cli-tool runner for deterministic local evidence when graph receipt sealing is unavailable on Windows.

verification.hosted_expected: Run runx harness ./skills/reply-router --json on a Linux host with receipt signing variables.
verification.local_limitation: Windows receipt store fails before a receipt id is written.
